insight: Holding Arka (6 defense, 4 attack) instead of clicking it is better than attacking with it, because the 7 defense tempo is immediate and valuable, allowing you to skip buying walls and buy attackers instead; clicking it only deals 4 damage half a turn later.
author: cooledcannon, jamberine, shadourow
context: Debate about whether to hold or click Arka, with analysis of defense vs attack tradeoffs.

insight: In high inflation scenarios, defense becomes progressively better relative to attack: 7 defense is much better than 4 attack, 6 defense is significantly better than 4 attack, but 3 defense is only somewhat better than 1 attack because the tempo advantage diminishes at lower scales.
author: cooledcannon, velizar_
context: Analysis of relative values of defense vs attack at different scales during inflation.

insight: Lancetooth significantly outperforms Splitter in long games when there is no absorb denial; Splitter deals 2 damage per turn immediately but Lancetooth deals 0 early, catching up at turn 3 and pulling ahead by turn 4, making it better when the opponent overdefends.
author: spiritfryer, shadourow
context: Comparative analysis of Lancetooth vs Splitter cumulative damage curves.

insight: A single extra Drone in your economy has three possible uses: (1) buy granularity engineers, (2) buy additional engineers when you can't justify more tech (bad planning), or (3) justify additional tech when money is tight (bad planning). Only case 1 is correct; cases 2 and 3 indicate poor build planning.
author: masn6811
context: Discussion of how extra drones function in economy planning.

insight: Extra drones beyond what you need should not buy additional drones themselves; if you have enough extra economy to buy another drone, your build is poorly planned.
author: velizar_
context: Extension of drone economy planning discussion to note the anti-pattern of extra drones.

insight: Bloodrager typically ties with two Tarsiers when denying 1 absorb, but is superior when denying 2 absorb; without absorb denial Bloodrager has lower early threat but can be used in rare high-economy situations where you don't want to invest in more tech.
author: spiritfryer, shadourow, cooledcannon
context: Analysis of Bloodrager efficiency compared to Tarsiers in different absorb denial scenarios.

insight: Against Apollo, buying a single snipeable attacker per turn is the worst possible strategy because each one gets sniped with consistent timing; instead buy 2+ snipeable attackers per turn so snipes are diminishing losses, or buy unsnipeable units.
author: masn6811
context: Discussion of optimal purchasing patterns when facing Apollo snipes.

insight: Splitter is more vulnerable to Apollo snipes than Tarsier because Tarsier comes in pairs naturally and has an extra turn of protection; Splitter is the worst snipeable attacker to buy one-at-a-time against Apollo.
author: masn6811
context: Comparison of snipeable attacker vulnerability to Apollo.

insight: Cauterizer is poor defense against Apollo because Apollo only shoots Cauterizer when doing 2 damage, and shoots Splitter instead when doing 0 damage; Bullwhip is superior to Cauterizer at the same cost because it provides better soak.
author: masn6811
context: Analysis of defensive unit choices against Apollo pressure.

insight: When floating blue mana against Dynamo, buying Splitters causes your floating blue to cost 1 gold per Splitter; when buying 2+ Splitters per turn the cost becomes linear and diminishing losses from snipes make this efficient.
author: masn6811
context: Discussion of Splitter purchasing efficiency with Dynamo in play.

insight: Colossus can be purchased just for the blue sink (Clunk) value when you need to avoid floating blue, and buying Colossus for blue absorption is sometimes correct even in Splitter-focused games; this strategy can backfire if opponent shoots the Animus.
author: masn6811
context: Discussion of unusual Colossus applications for blue sink purposes.

insight: In base set with Tarsier and Wall available, buying an unsnipeable attacker like Colossus is justifiable only if strong absorb/soak units like Doomed Wall are preventing the game from ending quickly.
author: spiritfryer, masn6811
context: Analysis of when inefficient attackers become playable due to game length and absorb availability.

insight: In Prismata's discrete turn economy, a half-turn drone advantage is worth approximately 6.93 gold equivalent in value due to drone multiplication, not exactly 7 gold, because gold grown over half a turn compounds at the (4/3)^0.5 rate.
author: spiritfryer
context: Mathematical analysis of second player drone advantage in continuous vs discrete economies.

insight: Redeemer + Innervi (14 cost, soaks 5 of 6 Redeemer gauss charges) vs Plasmafier (12 cost) tradeoff: Plasmafier is generally better in a vacuum because you don't sacrifice a drone or attacker, and the 2-gold premium for giving opponent a Gauss Charge (buildtime 3) is usually worth the extra attacker efficiency.
author: velizar_, shadourow, cooledcannon
context: Vacuum comparison of expensive red attacker options with soak synergies.

insight: Plasmafier is typically considered a strong attacker because it doesn't require sacrificing drones as fuel, while Redeemer (donger) is considered below average but becomes stronger when strong soak units are available.
author: cooledcannon, velizar_
context: Relative unit strength assessment for red attackers.

insight: When deciding between attack and defense: 7 defense now plus 4 absorb is significantly better than 4 damage half a turn later, because immediate defense allows buying more attackers next turn rather than walls.
author: cooledcannon, zera777
context: Discussion of fundamental attack vs defense timing principles.

insight: Buying a Blastforge is usually the correct play when you have excess gold and don't want to invest in additional technology, rather than buying inefficient attackers like Bloodrager.
author: shadourow
